title: Unable to start container due to mount problems
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

A toolbox container won't start due to storage mounting problem.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

No reproducer

**Describe the results you received:**

Output of `podman start --attach <container>`:

```
Error: unable to start container dc0174e8000608079db162af9b7e3dbca5e3bc5c24dfd43a88f949bfb5e868c0: error mounting storage for container dc0174e8000608079db162af9b7e3dbca5e3bc5c24dfd43a88f949bfb5e868c0: cannot mount layer 1d01a71c0ecfca182ed392f06b7888ba7fbf865b25a3abeb5e6f89bc6a459122: shifting not enabled
```

Output of `podman --log-level trace start --attach <container>`:

  <summary>Output</summary>

**Describe the results you expected:**

Successful start of the container

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I've had the container around for 2 months. Other containers are alright (the eldest 4 weeks old).

comments.body: @nalind PTAL - This looks like rootless Podman using native overlay - possible this is the whiteout issue?

@giuseppe PTAL
Is this an issue where fuse-overlayfs had builtin shifting and native overlay does not?

>  Is this an issue where fuse-overlayfs had builtin shifting and native overlay does not?

it could be an issue where fuse-overlayfs was used to create the container, then the storage switched to native overlay and we have lost the capability of shifting the layer at runtime.
How would one examine to see if they have fallen into this case?

could you force the use of fuse-overlayfs setting `mount_program`?

Alternatively, please try creating an empty file `/var/home/marty/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/.has-mount-program`

I accidentally purged the whole Podman state on my machine so I no longer have access to the affected container... 

> @HarryMichal, have the contents of the /etc/subuid or /etc/subgid files changed on your system since you started the container? Has your UID or primary GID been changed?

I added a new user some time ago but that involved a different set of problems and should not be related to this. The container was created after that change.
